title: Co-Founder Alignment & Compatibility

introduction.subtitle: Paired alignment assessment. 6 min per founder.
introduction.description:
Co-Founder Alignment & Compatibility helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind Ambition & Horizon, Roles & Decision Rights, Values & Conflict, Economics & Risk. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

The score stays separate from the optional answer-support check, which asks whether your answers are based on opinion, anecdotes, documentation, or system-verified evidence. A high answer should mean the practice is visible, repeatable, and useful in real decisions.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.duration: 8 diagnostic questions plus 4 evidence checks. Approximately 6 min per founder.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: Which outcome would make this company a success for you?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Ambition & Horizon; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Ambition & Horizon, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Ambition & Horizon has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Ambition & Horizon is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Ambition & Horizon: Which outcome would make this company a success for you
conceptHelp.summary: Ambition & Horizon means whether founders want the same kind of company, outcome, pace, and time commitment before conflict appears. This question narrows the concept to "Which outcome would make this company a success for you?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Founder alignment on ambition & horizon matters because hidden disagreement usually appears later as control conflict, resentment, slow decisions, or unfair expectations.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Answer from what the founders have explicitly discussed and would still accept under pressure. If the topic is only assumed, choose a lower option.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would mean the founders have explicitly discussed "Which outcome would make this company a success for you?", can name the agreed rule, and would still accept it when money, control, or conflict is at stake.

questions.text: Which decision model should the founding team use?

options.text: A. Authority is unclear, improvised, or concentrated in one person.
B. The practice is informal; boundaries, escalation, or records are inconsistent.
C. Owners, decision rules, and review points are documented for important cases.
D. Authority, evidence standards, exception handling, and periodic review are governed.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Roles & Decision Rights: Which decision model should the founding team use
conceptHelp.summary: Roles & Decision Rights means who owns which decisions, how roles change as the company grows, and how authority is handled before pressure rises. This question narrows the concept to "Which decision model should the founding team use?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

questions.text: Which financing and control strategy feels acceptable?

options.text: A. The area is mostly unmapped, reactive, or dependent on informal trust.
B. The area is known in broad terms, but controls, owners, or evidence are incomplete.
C. Owners, controls, thresholds, and response or remediation paths are documented.
D. Controls are tested, monitored, reviewed after incidents or changes, and integrated into operating governance.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.summary: Economics & Risk means how founders think about equity, dilution, control, financing, downside, and acceptable personal or company risk. contextQuestions.text: What level of evidence supports your answers for Ambition & Horizon?

options.text: Opinion: mostly judgement, memory, or individual perception.
Anecdotal: examples exist, but they are not systematically documented.
Documented: there are current notes, models, policies, metrics, or records.
System-verified: evidence is routinely produced by a governed workflow, system, or review cadence.

conceptHelp.title: Ambition & Horizon
conceptHelp.summary: This question asks how well your answers about Ambition & Horizon are supported. Ambition & Horizon means whether founders want the same kind of company, outcome, pace, and time commitment before conflict appears. The goal is learning: separate what you know from what you suspect, remember, or still need to verify.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: A result about Ambition & Horizon is more useful when you know whether it rests on opinion, a few examples, current documents, or system-verified evidence. Weak support does not make the answer useless; it tells you what to learn next.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Choose the strongest support you actually have right now. Do not upgrade an answer because the practice feels important, and do not downgrade it if current records or systems really exist.
conceptHelp.example: If your Ambition & Horizon answer is based on a recent metric, policy, deal document, customer record, or operating review, choose documented or system-verified. If it is mostly memory or judgement, choose opinion or anecdotal.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
contextDescription: This step is not scored. It records what your diagnostic answers are based on: opinion, anecdote, documentation, or system-verified evidence. Use it to decide how much to verify the result before acting on it.
